prompt: What's the current take on isometric training for maintaining strength on squat, bench, and deadlift when equipment is limited?
assertions: States the most effective isometric style is high-intent/ballistic maximal contractions
Notes strong angle specificity (gains transfer roughly 15-30 degrees from trained position)
Points out that isometric quarter-squat work won't strongly improve bottom-position squat strength
Mentions isometrics have a different fatigue profile (useful complement, avoids eccentric loading)
Notes that practical programming prescriptions for isometrics are less standardized
